extra.reasoning:
The property requires that globally (opted_out implies not sent) — i.e., no reachable state has opted_out=true AND sent=true. Transition [t5] is "on send_attempt if (opted_out and ticks >= 8): sent := true", which directly produces such a state. It is reachable within the 12-event bound.

Trace from initial state (opted_out=F, sent=F, ticks=0):
1. optout [t1]: opted_out=T, sent=F, ticks=0.
2-9. tick x8 [t4]: each increments ticks (capped at 8) and sets sent=F. After 8 ticks: opted_out=T, sent=F, ticks=8.
10. send_attempt: guard of [t5] (opted_out and ticks>=8) holds. [t5] fires, setting sent=true. Resulting state: opted_out=T, sent=T.

This state has opted_out=true and sent=true, violating globally(opted_out implies not sent). Total events = 10, within the 12-event bound. Note: on this send_attempt both [t3] (guard opted_out) and [t5] (guard opted_out and ticks>=8) are enabled; the spec assigns no priority, so [t5] firing is a valid nondeterministic execution. Hence the property is violated.
